title: Maesa macrophylla
taxonomy: Maesa macrophylla is classified as a species within the family Primulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the primrose or primulaw family. This family is part of the order Ericales, a larger group of flowering plants. The genus Maesa is also part of the family Primulaceae, and it is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. Maesa macrophylla is a member of the genus Maesa, characterized by its large leaves and woody stems. It is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of plants.

intro: Maesa macrophylla, also known as the 'long-leafed maesa' or 'long-leafed maesa tree', is a plant species within the family Primulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
